claims.text: In 1966, McDonnell Douglas engineer Dr. Chan Thomas wrote a 57-page book called 'The Adam and Eve Story' predicting an imminent civilization-ending Earth pole shift; the CIA classified it for roughly 60 years and only released a redacted version in recent years.

evidence.quote:
in 1966 a well-known scientist released
a book with information that could
impact everyone on Earth but before
anyone could read it it was classified
by the CIA we only learned about it a
few years ago when it was Declassified

in 1966 former McDonald Douglas
engineer Dr Chan Thomas released a book
called The Adam and Eve story

claims.rationale: Chan Thomas (real name Chauncey Powers Thomas) really did write 'The Adam and Eve Story' (1963/1966) and the CIA's CREST archive really does contain a copy classified in 1966 and partially declassified in 2013. The 'CIA hid the content because it was dangerous' framing is the popular reading; the more boring (and likely correct) explanation is laid out by the host himself later in the video — the book was a routine case-officer attachment, and only one document was classified, not a 'banned book' campaign.

claims.text: Thomas's central thesis is that catastrophic Earth pole shifts are cyclical: every several thousand years the Earth's axis rotates by up to 90 degrees in less than a day, killing nearly all life and forcing humanity to restart from the Stone Age — making us, he claims, the sixth advanced civilization to evolve.

evidence.quote:
Thomas presents evidence of a
coming shift in the Earth's poles that
creates a cataclysm and Thomas says
these pole shifts are cyclical that on
regular intervals a disaster wipes out
almost all human life and we start over
again in the Stone Age

Dr Chan Thomas in his book
The Adam and Eve story says the shift
happens in less than a day

claims.rationale: There is no geophysical mechanism by which Earth's rotational axis can flip 90 degrees in under a day — the angular momentum is too vast. The lithosphere is mechanically coupled to the mantle and cannot 'slide free' as Thomas describes. The 'sixth advanced civilization' claim is unsupported by the archaeological record. The pole-shift catastrophism Thomas inherits from Hapgood is rejected by mainstream geology, which has shown true polar wander is real but operates over millions of years at fractions of a degree per million years.

claims.text: Thomas's pole-shift mechanism is borrowed from Charles Hapgood's 1958 book 'The Earth's Shifting Crust' — for which Albert Einstein wrote a foreword — but Hapgood himself later issued a follow-up book retracting the mechanism after working with Einstein and other scientists; Thomas ignored the retraction.

evidence.quote:
in 1958 almost 10
years before the Adam and Eve story
Charles hapgood published the Earth's
shifting crust

in fact
Albert Einstein wrote the forwards for
two of his books

but he's
cherry-picking his favorite Parts
without giving specifics for example his
theory about the ice caps knocking the
Earth off axis well that comes from
hapgood's first book but after working
with Scientists including Albert
Einstein hapg good issued a follow-up
book that explains how he got that wrong

claims.rationale: Hapgood's 'Earth's Shifting Crust' (1958) and Einstein's foreword are real historical fact. Hapgood's later 'The Path of the Pole' (1970) significantly revised his earlier mechanism after correspondence with Einstein and others. Thomas's 1966 (and 1993 expanded) version did not incorporate these revisions. The host's reading is well-supported by the textual record.

claims.text: The Piri Reis map of 1513, which appears to depict the coastline of Antarctica more than three centuries before Antarctica was officially discovered in 1820, is offered as evidence that an ice-age civilization mapped the polar regions before the last hypothesized pole shift.

evidence.quote:
the per Rees map is something
that has baffled scientists for hundreds
of years on the PES map is the continent
of Antarctica but Antarctica wasn't
discovered until 1820 and per Reese
claimed the information on his map came
from much older Maps

claims.rationale: The Piri Reis map (1513) is real and Piri Reis did claim it was compiled from older sources. However, the 'Antarctica' interpretation is contested: mainstream cartographic historians (e.g., Gregory McIntosh's 'The Piri Reis Map of 1513') argue the southern landmass is a stylized rendering of South America's coast or the hypothetical Terra Australis Incognita that pre-Antarctica cartographers routinely drew. The 'ice-free Antarctica' interpretation requires assumptions that don't match the map's geography. Plausible-sounding but heavily disputed.

claims.text: Thomas predicted the most recent global flood was only 6,500 years ago — far more recent than the mainstream 11,500-year-ago Younger Dryas date — and recent archaeological discoveries of the Gun-Yu flood layer in China and submerged sites in the Mediterranean and Black Sea date floods to 6,500–7,000 years ago, vindicating that part of his timeline.

evidence.quote:
evidence of a great
flood has been found in the
Mediterranean and in the Black Sea these
discoveries put the flood between 6,500
and 7,000 years ago

claims.rationale: The Black Sea Deluge Hypothesis (Ryan & Pitman 1997) does propose a catastrophic Mediterranean → Black Sea inundation around 5600 BC (~7600 years ago, slightly older than the host's 6,500 figure). The Chinese 'Great Flood' under Yu the Great is dated to ~1920 BC by Wu et al. (Science, 2016) — much more recent than 6,500 years. So 'a flood' in that window is real, but conflating multiple regional flood events into a single global pole-shift catastrophe is unsupported. Partial vindication only.

claims.text: Erosion patterns at the base of the Great Sphinx of Giza show characteristic vertical-fissure water-erosion features, suggesting the monument was built before the last hypothesized cataclysmic flood (6,500 years ago) and therefore predates mainstream Egyptian civilization by thousands of years.

evidence.quote:
when we look at the erosion around the
base of the Great Sphinx we see patterns
that were formed by running water

claims.rationale: The Sphinx water-erosion hypothesis (Robert Schoch / John Anthony West) is a real fringe-academic position dating the Sphinx to 7000–9000 BC based on weathering on the enclosure walls. Most Egyptologists and geologists (Lehner, Hawass, Harrell) reject it, attributing the weathering to rainfall during the wetter period at the start of the Old Kingdom and to flaking from salt crystallization. The hypothesis is not refuted but is far from mainstream-accepted. Listing it as 'evidence for' a global cataclysm is overreach.

claims.text: The Easter Island moai statues — far from being just heads — are full bodies buried up to their necks, leading to two possible explanations: either ancient builders intentionally buried 30-foot, 80-ton statues to that depth, or a catastrophic mud flood buried them after the fact.

evidence.quote:
on Easter
Island are the M statues and you've seen
these it's lots of heads about a
thousand actually and most people don't
realize that these statues have bodies
buried deep underground

claims.rationale: The moai do have full bodies buried below ground; this was confirmed by the Easter Island Statue Project's excavations (2010s). However, the burial is well-explained by ~500 years of natural sediment accumulation from the volcanic Rano Raraku quarry slope plus deliberate partial burial during installation — not by a sudden mud flood. The first half of the claim is true; the 'evidence of cataclysm' framing is unsupported by archaeology.

claims.text: In a 1993 expanded edition, Chan Thomas updated his prediction to specify that the next pole shift would occur within 7 years — i.e., around the year 2000 — citing Nostradamus and Edgar Cayce as having made the same forecast; that prediction window has now passed by more than two decades with no such event.

evidence.quote:
Chan
Thomas updated his prediction in 1993
and said the shift would happen in 7
years around the year 2000

claims.rationale: Thomas's 1993 expanded edition does include this updated prediction; the year 2000 has long passed without any global pole shift, civilization collapse, ocean transgression, or magnetic flip. This is a clean empirical falsification of the specific dated prediction. The host correctly notes 'we're due' as the legacy framing but the temporal point is unambiguous: the prediction failed.

dependencies.rationale: A specific failed dated prediction undermines the broader cyclical-cataclysm thesis.

claims.text: Several auxiliary claims of Thomas's are corroborated by mainstream science: large earthquakes do measurably shift Earth's rotational axis (the 2004 Indonesian quake moved the pole ~25 cm), Younger Dryas meltwater pulses really did create 1,000-foot tsunamis and raise sea levels hundreds of feet, and woolly mammoths really have been found flash-frozen mid-meal.

evidence.quote:
NASA found that the big Indonesian
earthquake in 2004 shifted the North
Pole about 25 cm East

claims.rationale: NASA/JPL did calculate that the 2004 Sumatra-Andaman earthquake shifted the figure axis of Earth by approximately 2.5 cm and shortened the day by 2.68 microseconds (the host's 25 cm figure is off by an order of magnitude — likely confused mm/cm). Younger Dryas catastrophic floods (e.g., Lake Agassiz drainage) are well-attested. Frozen-mammoth specimens with vegetation in their stomachs (Berezovka, Yukagir) are real. Most of these auxiliary facts check out, even though they don't actually support a 90-degree pole flip.

claims.text: Earth is genuinely overdue for a magnetic pole reversal — the geomagnetic field reverses on average every 300,000 years and has been stable for ~800,000 — and the resulting weakened magnetic field could threaten power grids, satellites, and elevate cancer rates from solar storms; but a magnetic reversal is not the geographic-axis flip Thomas describes, and the geological record shows no mass extinctions during past reversals.

evidence.quote:
now the shifting magnetic
poles that is a concern we know the
Earth's poles flip-flop North becomes
South and vice versa and geologically
speaking it happens a lot it's happened
hundreds of times on average the time
between shifts is about 300,000 years
but we haven't shifted in almost 800,000
years so we're way overdue

the pole is now
drifting at almost 40 m per year and the
Earth's magnetic field is weakening
quickly these are signs that a shift
could be coming

claims.rationale: Geomagnetic reversals do happen on the order of every several hundred thousand years (the average is closer to ~200,000 years over the last 5 Myr; the host's 300k figure is in the right ballpark). The last full reversal (Brunhes-Matuyama) was 780,000 years ago, so 'overdue' is colloquially fair though the next one is not predictable to within tens of thousands of years. The North Magnetic Pole is indeed accelerating (recently re-modeled by NOAA/BGS for the World Magnetic Model). Power-grid and satellite vulnerability during weakened field epochs is real. Crucially, paleontological data shows no mass extinctions correlated with past reversals — the host gets this right and uses it to defuse the doom narrative.

dependencies.rationale: Magnetic reversal is not the geographic-axis cataclysm Thomas describes; conflating the two is the core error of the book.
